title: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV
display_title: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV
wikitext:
[]: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Sun Sep 15, 2024 2:28 pm For all the trauma he had to endure, I thought Shinji should have his revenge on everyone at NERV: Misato, Asuka, Ritsuko, and especially his poor excuse of a father Gendo. After everything he went through, I think he should have a sense of satisfaction of tearing down the whole NERV headquarters with his EVA.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Mon Sep 16, 2024 9:43 am Most of those working at NERV are in the dark about the secrets of the angels and are just working to prevent the Angel's attack and third impact. It's about the greater cause and Shinji is quite obedient when it comes to following orders from seniors, it was just that he was unfairly given such a huge responsibility. I can't see any fault in Misato, who offered to live with him so that he wouldn't be alone.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by Voyager » Fri Oct 04, 2024 8:35 pm Personally I could never hold it against him for wanting to destroy NERV after the dummy plug forced him to incapacitate Toji. The dummy system is proof that NERV never truly needed him as a pilot; but merely as a catalyst for the Third Impact. What better way to rob a child of his self confidence than force him to harm the one who gave it to him?

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV? B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 05, 2024 1:41 am Voyager wrote: Personally I could never hold it against him for wanting to destroy NERV after the dummy plug forced him to incapacitate Toji. The dummy system is proof that NERV never truly needed him as a pilot; but merely as a catalyst for the Third Impact. What better way to rob a child of his self confidence than force him to harm the one who gave it to him?

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV? B/             Quote Post by Voyager » Sun Oct 06, 2024 12:42 am Alaska Slim wrote: Voyager#943857 wrote: Personally I could never hold it against him for wanting to destroy NERV after the dummy plug forced him to incapacitate Toji. The dummy system is proof that NERV never truly needed him as a pilot; but merely as a catalyst for the Third Impact. What better way to rob a child of his self confidence than force him to harm the one who gave it to him?

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Oct 10, 2024 4:48 pm whom would that help? Certainly not Shinji. He would instantly regret it, just like when he took 'vengeance' on Asuka. I get the appeal of revenge as a power fantasy, but EVA isn't really a 'power fantasy' type of show. If anything it's kind of a 'power horror' where Shinji mostly agonizes over having terrible uwanted responsiblity put on him.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Thu Oct 10, 2024 5:11 pm Kendrix wrote: He would instantly regret it, just like when he took 'vengeance' on Asuka.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Oct 10, 2024 5:17 pm Didn't Shinji try to do this in NTE after Gendo tried to make him kill Asuka? That didn't help him much. Shinji found happiness despite NERV and the EVAs in the end anyway...

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 11, 2024 3:02 pm C.T.1290 wrote: Kendrix#943890 wrote: He would instantly regret it, just like when he took 'vengeance' on Asuka.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Mon Dec 30, 2024 9:50 pm Kendrix wrote: C.T.1290#943892 wrote: Was that during Instrumentality?

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Dec 31, 2024 4:28 am Even if he wanted to it was impossible. That's probably part of why he lashed out--maybe it was even a bid to test how far he could get. It wasn't far. That feeling of entrapment and lack of autonomy is why he ended up in that frame of mind--that revenge wasn't even plausible. As for the fap scene, I don't see that as revenge. It's a kind of lashing out, but it's pretty specific. Shinji's main mode is obedience, even when he goes on his sojourns or has self-destructive lapses. Think about the fap scene--he's getting away with the most private, invisible kind of retaliation possible. That's how buttoned up and resigned he is to those around him. I'd wager to say revenge is the exact opposite tendency that informs his character. The reason why the pivotal, shocking, affective moment in EoE is him choking Asuka isn't just about his feeling for Asuka, it's about his sense of himself as someone incapable of having any kind of easy escape route. When intimacy becomes impossible, the only human contact left is violence. He's someone constantly suppressing and avoiding confrontation so of course it's a pot that boils over. But there would be no satisfaction in the revenge, and it wouldn't really be revenge, it's more like desperation.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by viperzero » Tue Dec 31, 2024 3:37 pm Shinji has no reason to take revenge, he quits twice and no one stops him. Gendo played on his heart strings to get him to pilot the first time but he was never forced to pilot an Eva. He haded it but wanted to do it for the praise and sense of value it brought him. He’s afraid to quit because he knows this. The lashing iut and vengeance he does in EoE is his own childishness and selfishness. If he really wanted out he should have gone back to his teacher.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Dec 31, 2024 5:54 pm viperzero wrote: Shinji has no reason to take revenge, he quits twice and no one stops him. Gendo played on his heart strings to get him to pilot the first time but he was never forced to pilot an Eva. He haded it but wanted to do it for the praise and sense of value it brought him. He’s afraid to quit because he knows this. The lashing iut and vengeance he does in EoE is his own childishness and selfishness. If he really wanted out he should have gone back to his teacher.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by viperzero » Tue Dec 31, 2024 7:52 pm The ending is very much that he does have a choice. The lense in how he sees himself and the world can be changed. He obviously grew up neglected and never thought much of himself. Then he realized the praise and validation made him feel good so he stuck around even though he disliked it. Never didn’t make him stay and Gendo and Misato besides needing him also were driven by the same thing and couldn’t steer him. Shinji’s dependency and all the characters dependency is the driving force of the story. It’s only realizing were his myopic world and self view came from that allows him to change Nerv never had much to do with it.

[]: Re: Does anyone think that Shinji should have had his revenge on NERV?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Dec 31, 2024 8:12 pm viperzero wrote: The ending is very much that he does have a choice.

After everything he went through, I think he should have a sense of satisfaction of tearing down the whole NERV headquarters with his EVA.
